definition: Ignorant wanting directed toward sensual pleasures and agreeable sensory experience.
notes: The project prefers ignorant wanting for sensuality so the compound inherits the house rule for taṇhā while staying explicit about kāma. Sensual craving and craving for sensuality remain acceptable alternates when continuity with familiar Buddhist-English terminology matters.

context_rules.context: In prose where a shorter noun phrase reads better

example_phrases.notes: One of the classic threefold forms of craving.
